FULL CONTENT:
![RNA-Seq Blog Logo](https://www.rna-seqblog.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/New-logo-588x90-1.jpg)![](https://www.rna-seqblog.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/ILL-1027_Single-cell_ebook-banners_static_728x90_B_nohyphen.jpg)

## CD Genomics unveils groundbreaking targeted RNA sequencing platform

CD Genomics, a recognized leader in the field of genomics and transcriptomics, has proudly announced its refined service for [Targeted RNA Sequencing](https://www.cd-genomics.com/targeted-rna-sequencing.html). This novel technology enables researchers to obtain comprehensive RNA insights precisely and cost-effectively.

Targeted RNA-Seq is a ground-breaking approach to transcriptomics that allows for highly specific and deep analysis of RNA molecules in a targeted region or regions of interest. Unlike conventional transcriptomic studies that seek an all-inclusive approach to studying gene expression, targeted RNA sequencing zeroes in on specific transcripts or regions of interest. This refined focus provides researchers with a wealth of accurate data, without extraneous noise from areas that are irrelevant to the study at hand.

CD Genomics has decades of experience in genomic service, providing reliable and fully customizable solutions. Their targeted RNA sequencing services are designed to provide a high degree of sensitivity and specificity, providing a more precise way to study gene expression, fusion genes and alternative splicing in targeted regions.

“Targeted RNA-Sequencing can make previously difficult or impossible research feasible,” said the Chief Scientist at CD Genomics. “Its potential applications range from cancer research, where it can be used to detect rare mutations, to the study of cellular processes such as immunity and apoptosis. With its high degree of specificity, it can provide much more relevant and actionable data than other methods.”

He also added, “Through our targeted RNA sequencing services, we enable researchers to sequence only the specific set of genes they are interested in, achieving precise and comprehensive data for their studies. Furthermore, our targeted RNA-Seq service can sequence the same target area repeatedly, providing a detailed view of the changes over time.”

Additionally, CD Genomics provides data analysis, which includes raw data quality control, mapping of clean reads to reference genome, gene expression level estimation, alternative splicing analysis, and prediction of novel genes. Customers can rely on CD Genomics for a start-to-finish solution for their targeted RNA-Seq needs.

The launch of the refined Targeted RNA Sequencing service aligns with CD Genomics’ strategic focus on innovation and enhancing its service portfolio. This effort will allow researchers to deepen their understanding of gene expression profiles and contribute valuable insights to the fields of genomics and [transcriptomics](https://www.cd-genomics.com/transcriptomics.html), taking another significant step towards precision medicine.

These new advancements in Targeted RNA Sequencing services reflect CD Genomics’ unwavering commitment to providing the most reliable, highest-quality genomic services in the industry.

**Source** – [Pharmiweb.com](https://www.pharmiweb.com/press-release/2024-01-23/cd-genomics-unveils-groundbreaking-targeted-rna-sequencing-platform)

--- SOURCE 2: Case Study ---
URL: https://www.cd-genomics.com/case-study.html

SUMMARY:
Visit our case studies to learn how we use high-quality sequencing services to help customers' scientific research.

FULL CONTENT:
![CD Genomics](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/img/logo-w.svg)
![Sample Submission Guidelines](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/img/top-dna.svg)
![Sample Submission Guidelines](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/img/top-dna1.svg)
![banner](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/img/servicesbg.jpg)

# Case Study

![](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/images/application-spotlights-img1.jpg)

### Characterizing Complete Plasmid DNA Sequencing Analysis from P. larvae Tetracycline-resistant Strains

Plasmid-mediated gene transfer plays an important role not only in the mobilization and dissemination of antibiotic resistance and virulence genes but also in the evolution of microbial populations. CD Genomics offers complete plasmid DNA sequencing service and improved bioinformatics pipelines that deliver reliable and accurate data.

![](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/images/application-spotlights-img2.jpg)

### Targeted Region Sequencing of Bipolar Disorder Risk Locus *SYNES1*

Bipolar Disorder (BD) is a chronic brain disorder characterized by recurrent episodes of mania and depression, and *SYNES1* is considered a locus loci for BD. CD Genomics offers an effective and affordable targeted region sequencing for accurately detecting genomic variants in targeted genes/regions.

![](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/images/application-spotlights-img3.jpg)

### CircRNA Sequencing of EBV- and KSHV-infected Tumor Cells

Before this study, it was not known whether circular DNA tumor viruses, like Epstein-Barr virus (EBV) and Kaposi’s sarcoma herpesvirus (KSHV) expressed circRNAs. CD Genomics offers circRNA sequencing for checking the presence of circRNAs in EBV and KSHV tumors and cell lines cells, and characterizing these circRNAs produced by viruses.

![](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/images/application-spotlights-img4.jpg)

### Whole Genome Sequencing of *Vir-1* Mutant of *Arbidopsis*

In humans, KIAA1429, a homologue of *D. melanogaster* Virilizer (Vir), combined with METTL3, is required for m6A writer activity in mammals. CD Genomics offers a versatile method, whole genome sequencing, for detecting mutations in *vir-1* mutant of *Arabidopsis thaliana*.

![](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/images/application-spotlights-img5.jpg)

### NGS Elucidates the Association between High-fat Diet and Preterm Birth

Preterm delivery is the major cause of perinatal morbidity and mortality. And the consumption of a high-fat diet can increase the rates of spontaneous preterm birth. However, the underlying mechanisms are still unclear. CD Genomics offers both 16S rRNA sequencing and RNA-seq to explore the association between high-fat diet and preterm birth.

![](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/images/application-spotlights-img6.jpg)

### Whole Genome Sequencing of *Zygosaccharomyces bailii*-Derived Hybrid Strain ISA1307

The yeast strain ISA1307, isolated from a sparkling wine plant, was formerly considered the *Zygosaccharomyces bailii* species. However, whole-genome shotgun sequencing in this work indicates that the strain ISA1307 is an interspecies hybrid between *Z. bailii* and a closely related species.

![](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/images/application-spotlights-img7.jpg)

### Transcriptome Analysis of Wild Relatives of Peanut under Drought and Fungal Infection

The major constraints to cultivated peanut (*Arachis hypogaea*) are drought and fungal diseases. Two wild relatives of peanut harbor resistances to drought or fungal infection. CD Genomics offers global transcriptome analysis to identify genes involved in responses to biological processes such as fungal diseases and drought.

![](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/images/application-spotlights-img8.jpg)

### Explore Customized Bioinformatics Services: Case Studies & Insights

Several real-world projects, including CAR-T cell IL-4 regulation, phage genome comparison, mitochondrial genome assembly, and mouse RNA-seq analysis, demonstrate practical applications of bioinformatics in diverse research areas. CD Genomics offers customized transcriptome and multi-omics analysis to support gene discovery and interpretation across complex biological processes.

![](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/images/application-spotlights-img9.jpg)

### RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, and CRISPR Screening: Powering CAR T-Cell Research

CAR-T cell approaches are limited by cell exhaustion, which impacts their effectiveness. Wild-type immune cells show resistance to exhaustion. CD Genomics offers RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, and CRISPR screening to explore gene expression and chromatin changes in immune system research.

![](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/images/application-spotlights-img10.jpg)

### Integrated Analysis of ChIP-seq and RNA-seq: Decoding Pancreatic Cancer Stem Cell Mechanisms

ChIP-seq and RNA-seq were used to study gene regulation in ABCG2⁺ L3.6pl tumorspheres. ChIP-seq identified PHF5A and PHF14 co-binding sites, while RNA-seq revealed gene expression changes after KMT2A inhibition. CD Genomics provided full-service support to advance cancer stem cell research.

![](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/images/application-spotlights-img11.jpg)

### Power Your Regulatory Discoveries with DAP-seq: A MYB–GLK Case Study

Chloroplast development is complex, and identifying key regulatory factors can be challenging. DAP-seq enables high-resolution mapping of transcription factor binding sites, revealing critical regulatory networks. CD Genomics provides DAP-seq, RNA-seq, and bioinformatics services to uncover gene expression and chromatin dynamics in plant research.

\* is a required item.

--- SOURCE 4: Applications of Targeted Region Sequencing in Human ... ---
URL: https://www.cd-genomics.com/applications-of-targeted-region-sequencing-in-human-disease-studies-and-clinical-care.html

SUMMARY:
This review mainly describes the use of target region sequencing in applications for human disease studies and clinical care.

FULL CONTENT:
![CD Genomics](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/img/logo-w.svg)
![Sample Submission Guidelines](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/img/top-dna.svg)
![Sample Submission Guidelines](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/img/top-dna1.svg)
![banner](https://www.cd-genomics.com/wp-content/themes/v1/img/servicesbg.jpg)

# Applications of Targeted Region Sequencing in Human Disease Studies and Clinical Care

**Introduction to target region sequencing**

Since the introduction of Sanger sequencing in 1977, genetic sequencing has been greatly improved with costs simultaneously falling. With the ability to rapidly produce large volumes of sequencing data, next-generation sequencing (NGS) enables researchers to obtain whole genome or targeted regions of samples. [Targeted region sequencing](/targeted-region-sequencing.html) is preferred by researchers and clinical doctors who focus on human diseases, which is based on the fact that genomics and epigenomics have identified a number of genes associated with disease. In a fast and economical manner, targeted region sequencing is able to obtain the complete catalog of disease genes, allowing us to see the differences among thousands of people to discover critical genes that cause human disease, such as cancer, heart disease, autism, and schizophrenia.

Targeted region sequencing combines both target enrichment and NGS technologies. You can view our article [Current Techniques for Targeted Region Enrichment](/current-techniques-for-targeted-region-enrichment.html) for a better understanding of target enrichment methods and recommended products. The available products on the market can meet the needs of diverse research. Alternatively, custom panels can be designed based on your ideas to specifically capture any genomic regions or genes of interest. In the following sequencing process, while whole genome sequencing only provides coverage of tens of fold, targeted region sequencing achieves coverage levels up to a 1,000-fold higher, enabling more confidential results. This article mainly discusses the use of target region sequencing in applications for human disease studies and clinical practice.

**Application of targeted region sequencing in studying human disease**

Most Mendelian disorders are caused by exonic or splice-site mutations that alter the amino acid sequence of associated genes. The causative mutations for genetic diseases are listed in the database of Mendelian disorders in humans (<https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/omim>). Linkage analysis in family members is a traditional strategy of identification of disease-associated mutation. However, many diseases are rare, which poses challenges for linkage analysis in which data collections from large families are needed.

[Whole exome sequencing (WES)](/whole-exome-sequencing.html) has been proved as a robust and effective technique in replacement of linkage analysis. It can discover new disease-causing variants and verify benign single- and multiple-nucleotide polymorphisms in human populations. Since 85% of disease-related mutations are found in the protein coding regions, WES is especially productive to study human diseases. Other advantages of WES include: (i) reduced sample numbers; (ii) the capability of identifying causative mutations in diseases with genetic and phenotypic heterogeneity; (iii) cross checking for benign SNPs; (iv) the identification of breakpoints in balanced chromosome translocations and inversions. WES and other targeted region sequencing methods, followed by verification from nonconsanguineous families and functional and immunolabeling examinations, can discover critical causative genes from small pedigrees.

**Application of targeted region sequencing in clinical sets**

Most clinical applications of deep sequencing concentrate on known mutations to generate clear interpretable reports. Therefore, targeted sequencing using a panel of disease genes suit this aspect of clinical studies. But clinical applications of targeted region sequencing are faced with several limitations. First, there is no clear regulatory oversight. Second, data analysis and NGS errors remain to be solved. Third, there are controversial ethical issues. Even so, targeted region sequencing has been widely used for both research and clinical use.

The quality and amount of nucleic acid template are vital for the success of target enrichment and deep sequencing. The clinical sample types generally include fresh or frozen tissue that often yield high quality DNA and samples that often yield low quantity and/or compromised quality DNA like FFPE (formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded) tissue, decalcified FFPE tissue, bone marrow trephines, smears, and fine needle aspirates (FNA), plasma, and circulating tumor cells. Some studies have proved the usability of these clinical samples for NGS. And choosing the DNA isolation approach most-suited for the sample type is important to implement NGS testing. Several regulatory agencies such as Association for Molecular Pathology (AMP), American College of Medical Genetics (ACMG), College of American Pathologists (CAP), and US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) have proposed guidelines for the validation, implementation, and performing NGS testing involved in clinical applications.

Another issue needed to be considered is the clonal heterogeneity of tumors which generates the presence of multiple somatic mutations. Some methods such as the comparison and filtering of germline variants can provide better identification of the tumor heterogeneity. Additionally, similar challenges are presented by formalin-fixed artifacts. Do and Dobrovic (2015) once summarized the nature and origin of these artifacts and proposed approaches to overcome them.

If you are interested in our genomics services, please visit our website: [www.cd-genomics.com](http://www.cd-genomics.com) for more information. We can provide a full package of [genomics sequencing](/genomics.html), including [whole genome sequencing](/whole-genome-sequencing.html), [whole exome sequencing](/whole-exome-sequencing.html), [targeted region sequencing](/targeted-region-sequencing.html), [mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) sequencing](/mitochondrial-dna-mtdna-sequencing.html), and [complete plasmid DNA sequencing](/complete-plasmid-dna-sequencing.html).

enriched_points.expanded_content: CD Genomics employs targeted RNA sequencing, focusing on specific gene regions to provide high sensitivity and specificity, enabling in-depth analysis for rare disease research.

Utilizing targeted sequencing, CD Genomics has significantly enhanced diagnostic rates by accurately detecting mutations associated with rare diseases, thus facilitating timely and precise treatments.
The advancements in targeted gene sequencing lead to actionable insights in clinical settings, influencing treatment decisions and enabling personalized medicine strategies for rare diseases.
Future applications of targeted sequencing include expanding gene panels, integrating AI for data analysis, and enhancing precision medicine approaches for various rare genetic disorders.
